description: Exercises the context `requires` capability gate (context_v3.requires) in every schema form: string, array, and object ({ commands, files, env }), in both met (context runs and PASSes) and unmet (context lands SKIPPED, never FAIL) shapes. All steps are non-driver (shell echo only) so met permutations pass on every platform. `node` is always resolvable (the runner itself is Node), `$HOME` expands on every OS (USERPROFILE fallback on Windows), and `PATH` is always set — so the met permutations are deterministic. The unmet permutations name a command/env var that cannot exist; asserting their SKIPPED outcome (with the unmet-requirements reason) is the focused it() in core-core.test.js.

tests.description: String shorthand: a resolvable command lets the context run.
Array form: every command present, AND-ed.
Object form: commands + files (with $HOME expansion) + env all met.
String shorthand naming a nonexistent command: context is SKIPPED, not failed.
Object form with an unset env var: context is SKIPPED, not failed.
